title: Reporting requirements for cannabis and industrial hemp licences - Canada.ca
content:
## Reporting requirements for cannabis and industrial hemp licences
If you are a cannabis or industrial hemp licence holder, you are required to submit a number of reports throughout the year to Health Canada.
The table below lists the reports that you may need to submit along with:
- details and supporting information related guides regulatory references
- regulatory references
- how to submit associated forms submission section in the Cannabis Tracking Licensing System (CTLS) contact information for submission
- submission section in the Cannabis Tracking Licensing System (CTLS)
- contact information for submission
- who must provide each report (by licence type)
Note : As of March 12, 2025, you're no longer required to submit or retain an annual report of promotional (advertising) expenses and activities. You still need to keep a sample or copy of any promotional materials for at least 2 years after the day on which the promotion ended. For more information on promotional activities, refer to Promotion of cannabis: Prohibitions and permissions .
Reporting requirement
Details and supporting information
Licence types Table 1 Footnote 1
Key investor report (required by the Cannabis Regulations )
(required by the Cannabis Regulations )
As a licence holder, you'll need to determine if you have key investors. You'll need to submit either a key investor report form or a key investor attestation. Information on which you'll need to submit is available in the Cannabis key investors guidance. Cannabis key investors Cannabis Regulations, section 241
As a licence holder, you'll need to determine if you have key investors. You'll need to submit either a key investor report form or a key investor attestation. Information on which you'll need to submit is available in the Cannabis key investors guidance.
- Cannabis key investors
- Cannabis Regulations, section 241
Submit either a key investor report form or a key investor attestation in the "Reporting" section of the CTLS.
Submit either a key investor report form or a key investor attestation in the "Reporting" section of the CTLS.
Cultivation Processing Sale for medical purposes
- Sale for medical purposes
By January 31 of every year
By January 31 of every year
Statement of cannabis revenue (required by the Cannabis Fees Order )
Statement of cannabis revenue
(required by the Cannabis Fees Order )
You must submit a statement of cannabis revenue outlining cannabis revenue (that is, the amount of revenue received from the sale of cannabis minus the amount you paid for cannabis), for the previous fiscal year. Table 1 Footnote 2 You must upload this information every year, even if your cannabis revenue was $0 or negative. Cannabis Fees Order , subsection 8(1) Cost recovery for the regulation of cannabis
You must submit a statement of cannabis revenue outlining cannabis revenue (that is, the amount of revenue received from the sale of cannabis minus the amount you paid for cannabis), for the previous fiscal year. Table 1 Footnote 2
You must upload this information every year, even if your cannabis revenue was $0 or negative.
- Cannabis Fees Order , subsection 8(1)
- Cost recovery for the regulation of cannabis
Upload your information in the "Reporting" section of the CTLS every year. Attest to the accuracy of information and submit.
Upload your information in the "Reporting" section of the CTLS every year. Attest to the accuracy of information and submit.
Cultivation Processing Sale for medical purposes
- Sale for medical purposes
By April 30 of every year
By April 30 of every year
Institution-wide cannabis research licence: Annual report (required by the terms and conditions on your licence)
Institution-wide cannabis research licence: Annual report
(required by the terms and conditions on your licence)
If you're an institution-wide cannabis research licence holder, you must submit an annual report of your cannabis research activities.
If you're an institution-wide cannabis research licence holder, you must submit an annual report of your cannabis research activities.
To get a copy of the Annual report form template, email sp-licensing-cannabis-licences-sp@hc-sc.gc.ca . Email the Annual report form to sp-licensing-cannabis-licences-sp@hc-sc.gc.ca . Include "[your licence number] IRL Annual report" in the subject line.
To get a copy of the Annual report form template, email sp-licensing-cannabis-licences-sp@hc-sc.gc.ca .
Email the Annual report form to sp-licensing-cannabis-licences-sp@hc-sc.gc.ca .
Include "[your licence number] IRL Annual report" in the subject line.
Research (institution-wide model only )
- Research (institution-wide model only )
By April 1 of every year for organizations that held a valid licence on December 31 of the preceding year. The reporting period for this report is from January 1 to December 31 of the preceding calendar year.
By April 1 of every year for organizations that held a valid licence on December 31 of the preceding year.
The reporting period for this report is from January 1 to December 31 of the preceding calendar year.
Monthly cannabis tracking reports (required by the Cannabis Tracking System Order )
Monthly cannabis tracking reports
(required by the Cannabis Tracking System Order )
You must submit a monthly report tracking high-level movement of cannabis throughout the supply chain. Cannabis Tracking System: Monthly reporting guide Cannabis tracking system resources Cannabis Tracking System Order, section 2(1)
You must submit a monthly report tracking high-level movement of cannabis throughout the supply chain.
- Cannabis Tracking System: Monthly reporting guide
- Cannabis tracking system resources
- Cannabis Tracking System Order, section 2(1)
Upload your information or submit a manual report entry in the "Reporting" section of the CTLS.
Upload your information or submit a manual report entry in the "Reporting" section of the CTLS.
Cultivation Processing Sale for medical purposes
- Sale for medical purposes
15 th day of every month
15 th day of every month
Monthly health care practitioners  reports (required by the Cannabis Regulations )
Monthly health care practitioners  reports
(required by the Cannabis Regulations )
If you have a sale for medical purposes licence, you're required to submit a monthly report. This report includes information on medical client registration, including details about the health care practitioner who provided the medical document. Cannabis Tracking System: Monthly reporting guide Cannabis tracking system resources Cannabis Regulations , section 297
If you have a sale for medical purposes licence, you're required to submit a monthly report. This report includes information on medical client registration, including details about the health care practitioner who provided the medical document.
- Cannabis Tracking System: Monthly reporting guide
- Cannabis tracking system resources
- Cannabis Regulations , section 297
Upload your information in the "Reporting" section of the CTLS.
Upload your information in the "Reporting" section of the CTLS.
Sale for medical purposes
- Sale for medical purposes
15 th day of every month
15 th day of every month
Notice of new cannabis product (required by the Cannabis Regulations )
Notice of new cannabis product
(required by the Cannabis Regulations )
If you plan to sell a new cannabis product (other than cannabis plants, cannabis plant seeds, dried cannabis or fresh cannabis), you must notify Health Canada. Notice of new cannabis product guide: Requirements under section 244 of the Cannabis Regulations Cannabis Regulations, section 244
If you plan to sell a new cannabis product (other than cannabis plants, cannabis plant seeds, dried cannabis or fresh cannabis), you must notify Health Canada.
- Notice of new cannabis product guide: Requirements under section 244 of the Cannabis Regulations
- Cannabis Regulations, section 244
Upload your information in the "Notice of new cannabis product" section of the CTLS.
Upload your information in the "Notice of new cannabis product" section of the CTLS.
At least 60 days before product is available for sale
At least 60 days before product is available for sale
Recall (required by the Cannabis Regulations )
(required by the Cannabis Regulations )
You must report voluntary recalls of cannabis or cannabis products. Cannabis voluntary recall guide Cannabis Regulations, section 247
You must report voluntary recalls of cannabis or cannabis products.
- Cannabis voluntary recall guide
- Cannabis Regulations, section 247
Submit: an initial report prior to starting the recall a risk evaluation a final report Email your information to compliance-cannabis-conformite@hc-sc.gc.ca .
- an initial report prior to starting the recall
Email your information to compliance-cannabis-conformite@hc-sc.gc.ca .
Cultivation Processing Sale for medical purposes Research
- Sale for medical purposes
Initial report: Before starting the recall and notifying all those affected by the recall Risk evaluation: Within 72 hours of providing the initial report Final report: Within 30 days after the day on which the recall is completed
Initial report: Before starting the recall and notifying all those affected by the recall
Risk evaluation: Within 72 hours of providing the initial report
Final report: Within 30 days after the day on which the recall is completed
Positive pesticide case (required by the terms and conditions on your licence)
Positive pesticide case
(required by the terms and conditions on your licence)
If you get any test results equal to or exceeding the laboratory's limits of quantification, you must report the results. Mandatory cannabis testing for pesticide active ingredients: Requirements Mandatory cannabis testing for pesticide active ingredients: List and limits
If you get any test results equal to or exceeding the laboratory's limits of quantification, you must report the results.
- Mandatory cannabis testing for pesticide active ingredients: Requirements
- Mandatory cannabis testing for pesticide active ingredients: List and limits
Email your information to compliance-cannabis-conformite@hc-sc.gc.ca .
Email your information to compliance-cannabis-conformite@hc-sc.gc.ca .
Cultivation Processing Research (when conducting non-therapeutic research with cannabis)
- Research (when conducting non-therapeutic research with cannabis)
Within 7 days after test results
Within 7 days after test results
Loss or theft (required by the Cannabis Regulations )
(required by the Cannabis Regulations )
If loss or theft of cannabis or cannabis products occurs, you must report the details . Cannabis Regulations , section 158 Cannabis Regulations , section 246
If loss or theft of cannabis or cannabis products occurs, you must report the details .
- Cannabis Regulations , section 158
- Cannabis Regulations , section 246
Loss or theft report form for cannabis Email to compliance-cannabis-conformite@hc-sc.gc.ca .
Loss or theft report form for cannabis
Email to compliance-cannabis-conformite@hc-sc.gc.ca .
Cultivation Processing Sale for medical purposes Analytical testing Research Cannabis drug licence
- Sale for medical purposes
- Cannabis drug licence
Within 10 days after loss or theft
Within 10 days after loss or theft
Serious adverse reaction (required by the Cannabis Regulations )
Serious adverse reaction
(required by the Cannabis Regulations )
If a consumer of your cannabis product experiences a serious adverse reaction, you must report the details. Cannabis adverse reaction reporting guide Cannabis Regulations , section 248.1 This also applies to serious adverse reactions in the conduct of non-therapeutic research on cannabis. Cannabis Regulations, section 248.2
If a consumer of your cannabis product experiences a serious adverse reaction, you must report the details.
- Cannabis adverse reaction reporting guide
- Cannabis Regulations , section 248.1
This also applies to serious adverse reactions in the conduct of non-therapeutic research on cannabis.
- Cannabis Regulations, section 248.2
Fill out and mail one of the forms below: Mandatory adverse reaction reporting form for industry Reporting form for the Council for International Organizations of Medical Sciences
Fill out and mail one of the forms below:
- Mandatory adverse reaction reporting form for industry
- Reporting form for the Council for International Organizations of Medical Sciences
Processing Sale for medical purposes Research
- Sale for medical purposes
Report all serious adverse reactions to a cannabis product within 15 days after becoming aware of the serious adverse reaction
Report all serious adverse reactions to a cannabis product within 15 days after becoming aware of the serious adverse reaction
Cannabis import or export shipment detail notification (shipment endorsement) (required by the Cannabis Regulations )
Cannabis import or export shipment detail notification (shipment endorsement)
(required by the Cannabis Regulations )
If you imported or exported cannabis for medical or scientific purposes, you are required to report it. Apply for a cannabis import or export permit: Cultivation, processing, or sale for medical purposes Cannabis Regulations , section 209 Cannabis Regulations , section 218
If you imported or exported cannabis for medical or scientific purposes, you are required to report it.
- Apply for a cannabis import or export permit: Cultivation, processing, or sale for medical purposes
- Cannabis Regulations , section 209
- Cannabis Regulations , section 218
Submit the shipment endorsement in the CTLS.
Submit the shipment endorsement in the CTLS.
Cultivation Processing Sale for medical purposes Analytical testing Research Cannabis drug licence
- Sale for medical purposes
- Cannabis drug licence
Import: Within 15 days after release of shipment Export: Within 15 days after the date of exportation of a shipment
Import: Within 15 days after release of shipment
Export: Within 15 days after the date of exportation of a shipment
Industrial hemp import or export shipment detail notification (shipment endorsement) (required by the Industrial Hemp Regulations )
Industrial hemp import or export shipment detail notification (shipment endorsement)
(required by the Industrial Hemp Regulations )
You must report import or export details related to industrial hemp seed or grain. Industrial Hemp Regulations, sections 20 and 23
You must report import or export details related to industrial hemp seed or grain.
- Industrial Hemp Regulations, sections 20 and 23
Submit the shipment endorsement in the CTLS.
Submit the shipment endorsement in the CTLS.
Import: Within 20 days after release of shipment Export: Within 20 days after the date of exportation of a shipment
Import: Within 20 days after release of shipment
Export: Within 20 days after the date of exportation of a shipment
Industrial hemp notice of cultivation (required by the Industrial Hemp Regulations )
Industrial hemp notice of cultivation
(required by the Industrial Hemp Regulations )
If you're a licence holder cultivating hemp, you must report the: hemp variety number of hectares GPS coordinate of the site land ownership or consent Industrial Hemp Regulations, section 27
If you're a licence holder cultivating hemp, you must report the:
- GPS coordinate of the site
- land ownership or consent
- Industrial Hemp Regulations, section 27
Upload your information in the "Reporting" section of the CTLS if you cultivated. This section allows you to report that no cultivation took place in the last year.
Upload your information in the "Reporting" section of the CTLS if you cultivated.
This section allows you to report that no cultivation took place in the last year.
Within 30 days after seeding
Within 30 days after seeding
Industrial hemp THC test results (required by the Industrial Hemp Regulations )
Industrial hemp THC test results
(required by the Industrial Hemp Regulations )
If you're authorized and cultivated industrial hemp for seed, you must report the test results for the purpose of determining the concentration of THC in the flowering heads and leaves. Industrial Hemp Regulations, subsection 29(2)
If you're authorized and cultivated industrial hemp for seed, you must report the test results for the purpose of determining the concentration of THC in the flowering heads and leaves.
- Industrial Hemp Regulations, subsection 29(2)
If you're cultivating for seed: Upload your information in the "Reporting" section of the CTLS.
If you're cultivating for seed:
Upload your information in the "Reporting" section of the CTLS.
Within 15 days after receiving THC results
Within 15 days after receiving THC results
Reporting requirements to Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) (required by the Excise Act, 2001 )
Reporting requirements to Canada Revenue Agency (CRA)
(required by the Excise Act, 2001 )
For information on requirements under the Excise Act , 2001 , refer to Cannabis duty: Completing a cannabis duty return .
For information on requirements under the Excise Act , 2001 , refer to Cannabis duty: Completing a cannabis duty return .
Disclaimer: This guide outlines reporting obligations to Health Canada but doesn't cover all notices required under the Cannabis Act and its regulations. Depending on your licence type and circumstances, you may need to submit additional notices to the Minister, local authorities, your province or territory, or professional licensing authorities. Additionally, the Cannabis Regulations impose reporting obligations to other authorities, including the police, and you may have further responsibilities under federal, provincial, or territorial legislation, such as reporting to the Canada Revenue Agency. It's your responsibility to familiarize yourself with all applicable requirements.
